title: Might giving Shinji a backbone negatively impact Asukas character
display_title: Might giving Shinji a backbone negatively impact Asukas character
wikitext:
[]: Might giving Shinji a backbone negatively impact Asuka's character?             Quote Post by OneShotPunch » Wed Jul 31, 2024 10:19 am A frequent point of criticism over Sadamoto's manga and particularly the Rebuilds is how weaker Asuka comes across in both. Asuka barely got focused on in the Rebuilds to the point her backstory was revealed at the last minute, while the manga has her spared from the Mass Production Evas but see Kaji in her Third Impact complementation. Not so coincidentally, both entries have Shinji be less of an shit compared to the main series. From Eva to EoE, we get who and what Asuka is. She's a mirror of Shinji in a lot of ways, while he succees in the Eva she goes through a downward progression before EoE had her achieve a state of happiness fighting the MP Eva. Asuka failed, but she tried, winding up in a superior position than Shinji at the end, looking down at him and calling him disgusting over the terrible things he had done to her over the course of the movie. Meanwhile, neither the manga nor the Rebuilds have Shinji fail her so throughly (okay yeah Asuka was hurt in the Bardiel incident, but that winds up feeeling more like a petty grudge by the end and is forgiven), but don't do much with her either. Maybe it isn't so much that Shinji saving her is a wrong decision, but that neither entry came up with an alternate dynamic to replace the original. What do you think?

[]: Re: Might giving Shinji a backbone negatively impact Asuka's character?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Fri Aug 16, 2024 8:02 pm I think the problem here is that 'Shinji being a hero' or 'saving her' is more of a subjective point of view than anything at this point. I have seen a lot of fans reflecting over the last third of Shin and having different opinions on his actions, especially towards Asuka. I think the same can be said for the manga, but in a smaller scale. I agree with you that her character was very tied up to Shinji's in the OG series and that might have weakened her in the other continuities. But my main take away is that Asuka in NGE was a very human character that was made for that show's tone and themes in particular. Both the movies and the manga don't go anywhere as deep with any character to be honest. Asuka, being originally this very relatable character with her own side story happening alongside Shinji's (special and, in a way, isolated from the rest of the series even) got hurt the most. That being said, if you watch the movies expecting to see Shikinami and not Soryu, you will enjoy her a lot more. She has a lot of subtleties people don't see.
